summary: DHH reflects on an academically rewarding year despite a lighter course load. He credits a philosophy class and reading Gerald Weinberg for revealing connections between economics, computer science, and organizational theory. He notes the semester is nearly over with just a project and exams remaining, and expresses hope that his final bachelor's year will be equally fulfilling.
key_points: Self-directed learning (Weinberg readings) proved as valuable as formal coursework
Philosophy helped reveal fundamental connections between economics, computer science, and organizational theory
Interdisciplinary thinking was already a core interest before DHH's professional career began
money_quotes: The fields of economy, computer science, and organizational theory have moved closer—revealed their fundamental similarities and cooperation.
key_insight: Early in his education, DHH was already drawn to the interdisciplinary connections between technology, business, and organizational design that would define his career.
